We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to cancel the stale checks and restore in the books the balances of corresponding cash and liability accounts; (b) adjust the above-noted reconciling items and errors based on competent documents; and (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate in locating the documents pertaining to said reconciling items.

Several deficiencies were observed during the cash examination conducted on the cash accountabilities of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer and other Accountable Officers (AOs), such as (a) delayed remittance of collections; (b) AOs not bonded; and (c) Collecting Officers not duly designated as such, inconsistent with Section 64 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; thus, may result in misappropriation and loss of government funds.

Records show that during the year, collections and deposits were reported and accounted in the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD), and deposited in the authorized government depository bank (AGDB).

However, audit disclosed some deficiencies as follows:
Delayed remittance of collections
Analysis of the RCDs for the period September 17, 2019 to November 18, 2020 disclosed that most remittances made by the collectors to the Assistant Municipal Treasurer were still delayed by a number days averaging to four days. Inquiry with the AOs revealed that delayed remittances emanated from their practice of remitting collections only when the whole booklet of Accountable Forms (AFs) was used-up/issued.
The aforementioned practice was in contrary to Section 29 of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units (MNGAS for LGUs), which mandates that, at the close of each business day, the collectors/tellers shall accomplish the Report of RCD in four copies and together with the duplicates of the official receipts issued, shall submit to the treasurer/cashier to whom the cash collected shall be turned over.
The practice resulted to a longer the time the AOs held their cash collections hence, increases its exposure to risk of loss or misuse.
Accountable Officers not bonded
Section 101 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445 provides that “every officer of any government agency whose duties permit or require the possession or custody of government funds or property shall be accountable therefor and for the safekeeping thereof in conformity with law; and every AO shall be properly bonded in accordance with law”.
Review of records revealed that several Collecting Officers were not duly bonded (see table below) in clear violation of the above cited provisions hence, exposing the cash collections to risk that defalcations or shortages and unrelieved losses may not be recovered.
Collecting Officers (COs)not duly designated
It was also noted that some collecting officers were not issued corresponding special order or designation to act as such. While the special/designation order was not necessary for collecting officers where collection of local revenues was inherent or part of their duties and responsibilities as stated in their respective appointment, we noted that the following personnel were doing collecting functions that are not among their mandated duties:
Failure to officially designate the foregoing personnel as special collecting officers is not in accordance with Section 64 of Presidential Decree No. 1445,  and allowed unauthorized personnel to have access on collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to submit a certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, stating the name  and the amount due for the year; (b) Municipal Accountant set-up RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the accounting period based on the said certified list of taxpayers; and (c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile their records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify the non-current portion of Loans Payable-Domestic to Current Liabilities in the statement of financial position at the end of the reporting period in observance of the accrual method of accounting; (ii) reconcile the loan balances with the Land Bank of the Philippines (lending bank) to establish the correct amount of the Municipality's loan obligations; and (iii) submit to DILG the adjusted report showing the correct amount of loans payable as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) require (i) the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; (ii) GS Officer maintain complete and updated property cards for all PPE purchased in prior and current year and ensure to update them regularly; and (b) create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment by type annually and submit reports thereon to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to (a) explain why payment of his salary should not be suspended in view of the pervasive delays in the submission of reports and accounts; and (b) henceforth comply with the timely reporting and submission of accounts and financial reports as required in Section347 of R.A. No 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive thru the Municipal ENRO to continue its efforts of implementing, formulating and adopting the best environment practices in ecological waste management as required under R.A. No. 9003 to ensure total protection of the environment and the general welfare of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Members of the Local School Board (i) secure certification from the DepEd Budget Officer that the CY 2018 charges to SEF are already net of the said year’s DepEd Budget; (ii) ensure that future plans and budgets for SEF are consistent with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 and other pertinent rules that may be issued henceforth; (iii) refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing his signature for payment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) establish the LCPC; (b) instruct the LCPC to (i) prepare a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation; (ii) continuously ensure that at least 1% of the Municipality’s annual IRA is attributed to child protection related activities and in the development of a child-friendly environment; and (iii) prepare accomplishment reports and other necessary documents required under the MC Nos. 2008-126 and 2012-120.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, the BAC and the contractors concerned to immediately submit the above documents to the Audit Team for a facilitated settlement of the P11,331,807.85 worth of infrastructure contracts entered into by the Municipality. Henceforth, ensure that the use of government funds are adequately documented in accordance with the standards set forth in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012, Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 DOLE Department Order No. 13 Series of 1998, and Department of Public Works and Highways Department Order No. 216 dated November 16, 2000.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the LDRRM Officer to monitor and evaluate performance on DRRM, as warranted, based on the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards on Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; (b) provide the full appropriation of not less than five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources to be set aside as the LDRRMF to support disaster risk management activities; (c) monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans to ensure the full implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs; (d) refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (e) require that the balances of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE be transferred to the special trust fund account in observance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) Municipal Engineering Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate among themselves, determine the causes of the late project implementation, and to act accordingly; and (b) evaluate and consider re-programming of the non-implemented continuing appropriation.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Mayor together with the HRMO and concerned heads of offices (a) ensure that procurement of JOs, casual employees and consultants are made within the bounds of R.A. No. 9184; (b) conform with CSC, DBM, COA and other pertinent regulations the hiring of JOs, casual workers and consultants; (c) monitor the actual accomplishments of said workers with the end view of determining whether or not their services are necessary in operations, and act accordingly; (d) provide the Audit Team with the documents found wanting in the previous payments made for wages, which we will evaluate; and (e) ascertain the authenticity of claimants through checking on the validity of signatures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the FPS to observe effective planning and close monitoring of the implementation of the Gender and Development programs, projects and activities pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Heads of Offices and the BAC Secretariat to (i) prepare an APP that shows all the information required under RA No. 9184; (ii) refrain from procuring goods on account; and (iii) ensure that all future procurements and the method are based on the approved APP; (b) BAC to ensure that (i) the required procedures and the documentary requirements for alternative modes and procurement are met; (ii) the certification of non-availability of stocks from the DBM-PS is secured before resorting to the appropriate alternative method of procurement; and (iii) the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format are submitted to the GPPB  within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) BAC-TWG to disqualify future bids whose key personnel and equipment were already pledged/used in other on-going projects;  (b) Municipal Engineer/Engineer in-charge of the projects to (i) monitor the simultaneous implementation projects by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of work; and (b) BAC strengthen its bid post-qualification procedures in procurements of infrastructure projects by taking into consideration the technical capabilities of the prospective contractors to scrutinize the contracts that are advantageous and favorable to the government and public to maintain credibility of its procurement process.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit regularly the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor vehicles; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all supporting documents for future fuel consumption are properly accomplished and submitted; and (c) instruct the concerned officer to submit to the Audit Team all Statements of Account/Billing Statements of CY 2017 transactions.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Local Chief Executive (a) look into the reason for, and act accordingly, to address the continued failure of the LGU in the timely submission of the contracts, POs, DRs, and other documents required by regulations; and (b) assign an official that will (i) monitor compliance with the pertinent regulations; and (ii) provide timely feedbacks thereon to the senior management.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal officials (a) exert extra effort to intensify collection of past due accounts including penalties and interest; and (b) require the full and faithful compliance by all parties with the contract/agreement.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue with reconciliations of records, adjust records as warranted prior to eventual remittance of the outstanding balance.

We commended the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer's Office in their continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of pag-IBIG fund in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679. We also recommend the Accounting Office to (a) verify the negative balance signifying over-remittance; (b) make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and (c) reconcile with the Pag-IBIG and the Municipal Treasurer for the negative balance.
We recommended that the Management continue to enforce the settlement of disallowances from liable personnel.

We recommended that:

a) We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to obtain appropriate authority for and comply with the requirements of maintaining bank deposits with a private bank pursuant to DOF Department Order No. 01-2015. If efforts exerted yield no results, the deposit should be immediately transferred to the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP).

We recommended that: 

a.) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher to (a) record items for adjustment in the books and (b) stale checks which should be cancelled and recorded as accounts payable.

b.)   We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

a.) We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with certified list of taxpayers with stated amount due and collectible for the year which would be the basis of the latter to set-up RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the accounting period. 


b.) We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records pertaining to balances of RPT/SET receivables.

We recommended that:

a.) strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and reporting of cash advances and exert prudent efforts to require immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances, especially those granted to elected officials.

b.) refrain from granting of additional cash advances to official/employee unless their previous cash advances were liquidated and limit the grant of cash advance to elected officials only for their travelling expenses and allowances; and 

c.) In case of patent disregard and refusal of the accountable officers in settling their obligations or accounts, the following remedial actions may be resorted to:
withhold the salaries and/or other benefits as provided for under Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
file appropriate charges/cases, if warranted.

We recommended that management:

a.) Management should strictly adhere with the provisions of the NGAS Manual and related COA Circulars in accounting and management of government properties. 


b.) Conduct physical inventory of government properties periodically and update/prepare the required reports of serviceable and unserviceable properties;


c.) The Municipal Accountant and Property Unit should maintain complete subsidiary records and Property Card to facilitate periodic reconciliation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment with the books of accounts; and

d.) The Accounting Unit should revisit the guidelines to correct estimated useful life of each PPE and effect the necessary correction on the depreciation provided for each PPE.

We recommended that management:

a.) We recommended that the LGU require the concerned officers to submit the copies of approved purchase orders, contracts and delivery receipts to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by the existing rules and regulations.

a.) We recommended that the Municipal Budget Office observe the budgetary requirements and limitations on annual appropriations for personal services as provided in Section 325(a) of the Local Government Code of 1991 and initiate austerity measure to sustain a balanced budget.

We recommended that:

a.) the Municipal School Board prepare a SEF budget and implement programs, projects and activities in accordance with Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 and Joint Circulars promulgated and issued by the Department of Education, Culture and Sports, Department of Budget and Management, and Department of Interior and Local Government; and
